Open Source AI.content: [Asosiy kontentga oʻtish](#main) * [Uzbekistan](/en/uzbekistan/) * [World](/en/world/) * [Society](/en/society/) * [Tech](/en/technology/) * [Sport](/en/sport/) * [Auto](/en/auto/) * [Culture](/en/culture/) * [Economy](/en/economy/) * [Ўзбекча](#) * [O'zbek](#) * [Русский](#) * [Türkçe](#) * [Қазақша](#) * [Français](#) * [Español](#) * [Deutsch](#) # Large-scale investigation into OpenAI launched in the US ·0·[Technology](/en/technology/) A coalition of attorneys general from several US states has launched an official investigation into [OpenAI](/en/tags/openai/). According to The Wall Street Journal, the New York Attorney General's office issued a subpoena last Friday for the company to provide relevant documents. This is reported by Techcrunch.com [reports](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA2LzEzL29wZW5haS1mYWNlcy1pbnZlc3RpZ2F0aW9uLWZyb20tc3RhdGUtYXR0b3JuZXlzLWdlbmVyYWwv) . The investigation is examining OpenAI's advertising practices, user acquisition and retention methods, the behavior of its AI models, and its handling of consumer personal and medical data. Special attention is also being paid to the safety of minors and the elderly. An OpenAI spokesperson confirmed that the company is cooperating with the investigation. According to them, [ChatGPT](/en/tags/chatgpt/) now offers a more protected experience for minors and includes special safety systems that direct users in difficult situations to reliable resources. However, the company did not disclose which specific states are involved in the probe. As a reminder, OpenAI recently won a high-profile lawsuit against one of its founders, Elon Musk. Nevertheless, the company continues to face numerous lawsuits regarding copyright infringement and various negative consequences caused by the ChatGPT platform. Recently, Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier also filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO [Sam Altman](/en/tags/sam-altman/), accusing them of ignoring safety warnings and putting millions of people at risk. At the same time, OpenAI announced this week that it has confidentially filed for an IPO. Open Source AI.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC
Open Source AI.titleZh: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC
Open Source AI.content: [Skip to content](#bbc-main) [Watch Live](/watch-live-news/) [Watch Live](/watch-live-news/) # Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns Harry Sekulich Anthropic has suspended its powerful new AI model after US authorities raised security concerns just days following its public release. [In a statement published on its website](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access), Anthropic said it was ordered to suspend foreign nationals from using Claude Fable 5, a program that the company self-described as "too powerful". "The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all our customers to ensure compliance," the company wrote. Anthropic and the Trump administration are involved in a separate ongoing lawsuit over an order to stop government agencies using the company's AI tools. The BBC has approached the US Department of Commerce for comment. Claude Fable 5 is a version of Anthropic's Claude Mythos, an AI program rivalling competitors OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's Gemini. Anthropic said US national security authorities had not identified specific concerns. "Our understanding is that the government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or 'jailbreaking' Fable 5," the company said. Jailbreaking is a process of getting past software restrictions designed to protect a cyber network, allowing hackers to access sensitive information or unblock features. "We reviewed a demonstration of this specific technique being used to identify a small number of previously known, minor vulnerabilities," Anthropic said. "These vulnerabilities all appear relatively simple, and we have found that other publicly-available models are able to discover them as well without requiring a bypass." Ahead of the release of Claude Fable 5, the company touted various "safeguards" it had implemented to prevent cyber hacking. Finance, technology and government leaders had expressed concerns about its public rollout, following a private release in April for previewing and testing vulnerabilities within its own system. Anthropic said it enabled pre-release access for a handful of organisations because the tool was so intelligent that it could be dangerous because of its ability to exploit or hack computer systems. The company self-proclaimed that it was "too powerful to release" before Claude Fable 5 was publicly released, which some critics questioned as inflated hype and marketing spin. "Fable's capabilities exceed those of any model we've ever made generally available," the company said. Anthropic has found itself in the crosshairs of the Trump administration recently. Donald Trump has criticised the company publicly and then US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth labelled it a "supply chain risk" – the first time a US company has ever publicly received such a designation. The designation means a tool or service is not secure enough for government use, and is historically reserved for companies based in adversarial countries. Anthropic is suing the Pentagon following the designation. A US judge has ruled the Pentagon's directive could not be enforced, meaning government agencies and organisations working with the US military can still use Anthropic while the lawsuit continues. [## Version of AI tool 'too powerful for public' released to public](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/ckg701v1dp6o) [## Finance ministers and bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2ev24yx4rmo) [## Judge rejects Pentagon's attempt to 'cripple' Anthropic](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cvg4p02lvd0o) [Artificial intelligence](/news/topics/ce1qrvleleqt) ---

Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email. [Become an Insider](/subscription) and start reading now. Have an account? . OpenAI said it's "committed to learning" after a coalition of states launched an investigation into how the tech startup's products impact users. An OpenAI spokesperson said in a statement that it's taking the states' concerns "seriously" and will "engage constructively with their offices." "Today's ChatGPT includes a more protective experience for minors and people experiencing difficult situations, with safeguards that direct them to real-world resources and trusted human contacts," the spokesperson said. "None of this changes what families have gone through, but we are committed to learning, improving, and getting this right," they added. New York State Attorney General Letitia James served OpenAI a subpoena on Friday seeking a wide range of documents, The Wall Street Journal first reported. The documents pertained to user engagement and retention, the company's handling of health and consumer data, deep learning models, activities related to young and older users, and more, the Journal reported. Friday's subpoena is the latest legal headache for OpenAI. The company has navigated [copyright infringement](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-lawsuit-copyrighted-data-train-chatgpt-court-tech-ai-news-2024-6) claims, [privacy lawsuits](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-chatgpt-generative-ai-stole-personal-data-lawsuit-children-medical-2023-6), and a high-profile trial pitting [SpaceX CEO Elon Musk](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-sam-altman-elon-musk-jury-trial-verdict-2026-5) against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. Perhaps most concerning, however, are [a handful of lawsuits](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-add-chatgpt-safeguards-teen-suicide-lawsuit-ai-mental-health-2025-8) that say ChatGPT contributed to decisions by users to die by suicide. In response to a May report by The New York Times, the company said ChatGPT "is not a substitute for medical or mental health care, and we have continued to strengthen how it responds in sensitive and acute situations with input from mental health experts." Play now](/games) The family of a victim in a fatal campus shooting at Florida State University in April, meanwhile, has also filed a lawsuit against OpenAI. Open Source AI.title: Saile Strengthens Position as AI-Driven Healthcare Workforce Infrastructure Provider - TipRanks
Open Source AI.titleZh: Saile Strengthens Position as AI-Driven Healthcare Workforce Infrastructure Provider - TipRanks
Open Source AI.content: Advertisement Advertisement # Saile Strengthens Position as AI-Driven Healthcare Workforce Infrastructure Provider [TipRanks Private Companies Auto-Generated Newsdesk](/news/author/tipranksprivatecopmaniesnewsdesk) [Saile](https://www.tipranks.com/private-companies/saile) – a private healthtech company focused on clinician staffing – featured prominently this week as it underscored its role as a “workforce infrastructure” platform rather than a traditional job board. The firm is building an AI-driven pipeline that integrates recruiting, primary source verification, credential file management, payer enrollment, onboarding, staffing, and compliance monitoring. ### [Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents](https://mcp.tipranks.com/?utm_source=tipranks&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=mcp_crosssell&utm_content=rss) * [Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.](https://mcp.tipranks.com/?utm_source=tipranks&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=mcp_crosssell&utm_content=rss) * Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows. Across recent disclosures, Saile reported its network generated 2,900 job interests from 704 engaged physicians over the past two months, with license and credential expiration data available for 93% of them. The company highlighted named customers such as CITYMD, Summit Health, SCA Health, Sollis Health, GoHealth Urgent Care, RadNet, and NYC Health + Hospitals, signaling traction with urgent care, outpatient, and enterprise-scale health systems. A major theme this week was automated credential and compliance management for clinician workforces. Over the past 90 days, Saile’s system sent 787 credential reminders to 108 providers, with an average lead time of 41 days before expiry, and the firm noted a database of 7,198 credential files, 85.8% of which are described as audit-ready. The platform tracks a wide range of requirements – including state medical licenses, DEA certificates, board certifications, BLS, and malpractice coverage – through a single dashboard. Saile also promotes capabilities such as PRN bench management, staffing pipeline oversight, and the removal of spreadsheet-based workflows, emphasizing day-zero onboarding and automated expiration alerts. Saile’s analysis of 1,150 workflow question instances in onboarding forms found that roughly 24.3% of questions duplicate information already collected elsewhere, with 120 prompts repeated across organizations. The company argues that such redundancy contributes to administrative burden and clinician burnout, and it advocates a “collect once, use everywhere” model that centralizes reusable, verified data like phone numbers, DEA history, clinical privilege history, gaps in practice, and disclosure information. Additional data from the platform suggests more than 5,700 verified clinicians are currently listed, with 69.8% open to per-diem and PRN roles and 32% willing to accept locum tenens work. Contract roles reportedly show the closest alignment between supply and demand on the platform, pointing to near-term opportunities in flexible workforce solutions, particularly in per-diem and PRN staffing. From a financial and strategic perspective, these developments position Saile as an infrastructure provider for healthcare workforce operations, targeting hospitals, urgent care centers, ASCs, telehealth platforms, MSOs, and home health agencies. If healthcare organizations adopt Saile’s tools to streamline onboarding, credentialing, and compliance, the company could see enhanced recurring revenue potential, deeper integration into administrative workflows, and strengthened competitive standing in healthtech and workforce management. While the week’s communications highlight operational metrics, customer logos, and platform capabilities, they offer limited insight into revenue, pricing, or retention metrics. Overall, the news flow portrays a company sharpening...

& IT](/business/sunday-business-it) # Nvidia, Korean firms expand AI infrastructure [By Ira James](https://www.manilatimes.net/author/ira-james "Ira James") June 14, 2026 Resize * Small * Medium * Large SEVERAL South Korean companies have announced expanded collaborations with Nvidia to develop artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, advanced semiconductors, robotics and cloud computing capabilities as demand for AI services continues to grow. The initiatives involve internet company Naver, telecommunications provider SK Telecom, semiconductor manufacturer SK hynix and industrial conglomerate Doosan Group, which are all pursuing projects aimed at supporting large-scale AI deployment across industries. Naver said it plans to expand its AI infrastructure using Nvidia's DSX platform, beginning with additional capacity at its GAK Sejong data center in South Korea. The company said the expansion will support AI services for businesses, government agencies and cloud customers, while also supporting the development of its AI models and services. SK Telecom announced plans to build a large-scale AI cloud platform in South Korea, with its first AI factory scheduled to begin operations in 2027. The company said the infrastructure will support enterprise, sovereign and physical AI applications and could eventually serve customers across Asia. Meanwhile, Nvidia and SK hynix unveiled a multiyear partnership to develop next-generation memory technologies for AI systems. Infra & Chips.title: Nvidia Stock Just Did Something for the First Time in More Than 5 Years. Here's What History Says Happens Next. - The Motley Fool
Infra & Chips.titleZh: Nvidia Stock Just Did Something for the First Time in More Than 5 Years. Here's What History Says Happens Next. - The Motley Fool
Infra & Chips.content: [Accessibility Menu](#) [▲ S&P 500 **+---%**|▲ Stock Advisor **+---%**Join The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/mms/mark/e-foolcom-sa-top-nav-returns) [Accessibility](#)[Help](https://support.fool.com/) So far this year, **Nvidia** ([NVDA](/quote/nasdaq/nvda/) +0.15%) stock has gained 8% -- placing it slightly above the returns in the **S&P 500** and nominally trailing those seen in the **Nasdaq**. From a valuation perspective, the world's most valuable company boasts a [forward price-to-earnings](https://www.fool.com/terms/f/forward-pe/) (P/E) ratio of about 22. Moreover, Nvidia's forward P/E has spent much of 2026 locked in a narrow corridor between roughly 18 and 25. This steadiness raises two questions: When was the last time investors saw Nvidia's forward multiple behave this way and what happened next? Image source: Nvidia. ## Nvidia's valuation profile echoes its pre-AI boom Per the chart below, investors can see that Nvidia's forward P/E has not traded inside a comparable, compressed band since before the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution. Prior to the outburst of [generative AI models](https://www.fool.com/terms/g/generative-ai/) back in late 2022, the market largely viewed Nvidia as a company primarily focused on graphics and gaming with a data center services side hustle. [NVDA PE Ratio (Forward)](https://ycharts.com/companies/NVDA/forward_pe_ratio) data by [YCharts.](https://ycharts.com) Once ChatGPT, Anthropic's Claude, and a handful of other frontier models arrived, demand for accelerated computing exploded. As it turns out, Nvidia's [first-mover advantage](https://www.fool.com/terms/f/first-mover/) in designing graphics processing units (GPUs) was uniquely positioned for this moment. Hence, the company's revenue and earnings rose dramatically virtually overnight. Subsequently, Nvidia's forward earnings valuation multiple broke out and spent the next few years oscillating at levels frequently above 40. The current range represents a reversion to a pre-AI boom rhythm. Against this backdrop, the current sideways trading seen in Nvidia is the first real extended stretch of valuation stability since the world began pricing the company as the indispensable king of AI infrastructure build-outs. ## Don't let valuation distract you from Nvidia's guidance Nvidia's valuation profile looks even more striking when set against the company's actual operating momentum. During the fiscal 2027 first quarter (ended April 26,2026), revenue from Nvidia's data center segment surged 92% year over year, reaching $75 billion. Management forecasted total revenue to be $91 billion next quarter, plus or minus 2%. This represents an acceleration quarter over quarter and a staggering 95% year-over-year growth. To me, a forward earnings multiple stuck around 22 does not necessarily signal skepticism about Nvidia's near-term earnings power. Instead, I think it reflects a situation whereby the market has already baked in meaningful growth and is no longer willing to pay the premium multiples witnessed throughout 2023 to 2025. ## [NASDAQ: NVDA](/quote/nasdaq/nvda/) Today's Change (0.15%) $0.30 Current Price $205.18 ### Key Data Points Market Cap $5.0T Day's Range $203.45- $207.06 52wk Range $142.03- $236.54 Volume 4.8M Avg Vol 165.3M Gross Margin 74.15% Dividend Yield 0.14% In other words, range-bound valuation multiples can be normal when investors simply expect continued strong top- and bottom-line expansion. These dynamics are actually quite common for mature, high-growth technology platforms. ## Nvidia is positioned for another leg of valuation expansion After Nvidia's forward P/E dropped sharply to about 18 earlier this year, the multiple has more recently stabilized and begun to edge modestly higher within a tight range. This could suggest that early stages of [valuation expansion](https://www.fool.com/terms/v/what-is-valuation-expansion/) are in the works. Infra & Chips.title: The power decisions that could shape the next century - Axios
Infra & Chips.titleZh: The power decisions that could shape the next century - Axios
Infra & Chips.content: [Skip to main content](#main-content) [Axios](/) 4 mins ago - [Energy & Climate](https://www.axios.com/energy-climate) # The power decisions that could shape the next century * [Amy Harder](https://www.axios.com/authors/amy.harder@axios.com) [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) Add Axios as your preferred source to see more of our stories on Google. [Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com) The AI-driven power boom is forcing a once-in-a-generation decision about how America's electricity system should grow. **The big picture:** For decades, utilities planned around predictable increases in demand. AI is changing that. **State of play:** Data centers now seek amounts of electricity that used to be associated with entire cities — raising questions about who pays for new infrastructure, who gets access to scarce power and how quickly projects can connect to the grid. **Driving the news:** Debates are unfolding at the nation's largest grid operator, PJM, and at the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission. * Some proposals would allow data centers to connect directly to power plants or generate their own power on site, at least initially operating outside the broader electricity grid. **Zoom out:** Those debates reflect a broader question facing regulators across the country: how to handle massive new electricity users. **What we're watching:** Multiple decisions will unfold over months and years, with a key decision by the federal agency expected [as soon as this month](https://www.ferc.gov/news-events/news/ferc-act-large-load-interconnection-docket-june-2026?utm_source=chatgpt.com). * The outcomes will influence electricity prices, reliability and the pace of AI development. **The bottom line:** Decisions now being made by regulators, utilities and technology companies could determine whether the AI boom accelerates a historic expansion of the electric grid — or creates a parallel power system alongside it. *This story is part of an Axios Deep Dive on the policy debates shaping America's future. Infra & Chips.title: Censia Deepens Workday Alignment to Advance Agentic AI Workforce Strategy - TipRanks
Infra & Chips.titleZh: Censia Deepens Workday Alignment to Advance Agentic AI Workforce Strategy - TipRanks
Infra & Chips.content: Advertisement Advertisement # Censia Deepens Workday Alignment to Advance Agentic AI Workforce Strategy [TipRanks Private Companies Auto-Generated Newsdesk](/news/author/tipranksprivatecopmaniesnewsdesk) [Censia](https://www.tipranks.com/private-companies/censia) used the week to sharpen its positioning as an AI-driven workforce intelligence provider, centering its messaging on deepening alignment with Workday’s ecosystem. The company framed its platform as a bridge from static talent profiles to dynamic, capability-based views that support redeployment and strategic workforce planning. ### [Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents](https://mcp.tipranks.com/?utm_source=tipranks&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=mcp_crosssell&utm_content=rss) * [Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.](https://mcp.tipranks.com/?utm_source=tipranks&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=mcp_crosssell&utm_content=rss) * Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows. Censia highlighted its role as an Innovation Sponsor at Workday Elevate San Francisco, an event focused on scaling agentic AI across HR, finance, IT, and operations. The sponsorship is aimed at expanding visibility with large enterprises and embedding Censia’s tools within complex Workday-centric environments. A key spotlight is the closing keynote at Workday Elevate San Francisco, where Monica Bua, President of Censia AI Executive Intelligence, will join Workday Chief Learning Officer Chris Ernst for a live podcast-style session. The discussion will address how agentic AI is emerging in enterprise settings and how leaders can design operating models that allow humans and AI agents to work side by side. CEO Joanna Riley also appeared on the Workday Partners Innovation Exchange Podcast to discuss how [artificial intelligence](https://www.tipranks.com/compare-stocks/artificial-intelligence) is reshaping workforce management. She underscored the shift from experimental pilots to production-grade AI deployments and emphasized the need for trusted workforce data as a foundation for AI agents in HR and planning. Across its communications, Censia stressed responsible and explainable AI, noting that transparency of recommendations and the preservation of human judgment are central to its approach. Governance, clarity, and accountability frameworks were presented as critical for organizations deploying agentic AI at scale. From a market perspective, Censia’s increased presence within Workday events and media channels may bolster its business development pipeline and support deeper integrations in the HR tech and talent intelligence ecosystem. By focusing on practical, revenue-oriented use cases and enterprise-grade implementations, the company is working to strengthen its competitive position and future growth prospects. Overall, the week underscored Censia’s strategy of leveraging Workday partnerships, thought leadership around agentic AI, and a focus on explainable workforce intelligence to advance its role as a key player in enterprise AI-driven workforce solutions.
